patches.summary: Cache key hashes Cookie/CSRF and other credential headers, not just the Bearer token.
patches.reason: Greptile P1/security: cookie-auth sessions shared a cache key because AuthHeader() returns only the Bearer token, so a re-login within the 5-minute TTL served the previous session's cached responses.

patches.summary: CheckRedirect strips all configured custom credential headers (x-csrftoken, etc.) on cross-host hops, not just Authorization.
patches.reason: Greptile P1/security: Go strips Authorization/Cookie cross-host but forwards custom headers verbatim, leaking the CSRF token to the redirect target.

patches.summary: Removed unused applyAuthFormat and its strings.ReplaceAll keep-alive guard.
patches.reason: Greptile: dead code kept compilable only by a 'var _ = strings.ReplaceAll' guard; strings is already used by Load().

patches.summary: SQLite store and HTTP disk cache (dirs/files/WAL/SHM) restricted to owner-only (0o700/0o600).
patches.reason: Same world-readable class Greptile flagged on godaddy: store/cache were created 0o755 dir + umask-default file, leaving locally cached account/session data readable by other local users. Hardened proactively to match the midjourney/godaddy reference.

patches.summary: maskCredentialText now masks Cookie/CSRF values from Config.Headers, not just the Bearer token.
patches.reason: Greptile P1/security: cookie-based Cookie and x-csrftoken values were emitted verbatim in APIError.Body, dry-run output, and displayed URLs.

patches.summary: store.List treats negative limit as unbounded; analytics/data_source pass -1 for the full dataset.
patches.reason: Greptile P1: analytics --group-by called db.List(...,0) which clamped to 200, producing wrong counts on datasets over 200 rows.
